prompts.prompt: Should I raise my Series A or stay bootstrapped?
Explain agentic commerce to me.
Is remote work better for innovation?
Connect originality to organizational structure.
How does Scott's thinking on AI agents evolve?
Surprise me with a high-confidence idea I probably haven't seen.
I'm deciding whether to pivot my product. What should I be asking myself?
What happens to SaaS if AI agents do most of the work?
Everyone keeps saying AI is coming for creative jobs, but I'm not sure that's the whole story. What's actually becoming more valuable in a world where AI can generate anything?
I'm three years into building my startup and it's nowhere near where I thought it would be by now. Honestly questioning whether to keep going. What's your honest take on this moment?
How should a 10-person startup think about competing with a 500-person company that has way more resources and brand recognition?
We're about to launch our product. What should we be obsessing over in the final weeks before it goes live?
expected_themes: users are lazy, vain, and selfish

prompts.prompt: Every founder I talk to says you need to find product-market fit through market research and customer interviews. Is that really where the best startup ideas come from?
expected_themes: annoyance as signal for creative opportunity

prompts.prompt: With AI doing so much of the execution work now, what should founders and leaders actually be spending their time on?
expected_themes: art of business is what doesn't scale

prompts.prompt: My investors keep pushing us to raise a bigger round so we can hire faster and move quicker. Should I take the money?
expected_themes: resourcefulness is muscle, resources are carbs

prompts.prompt: We're building a SaaS platform and trying to figure out our pricing model. Seat-based licensing seems like the obvious default — is that still the right call?
Given how fast AI is improving, isn't the best growth strategy just to scale up users and engagement as quickly as possible and figure out the sustainable business model later?
prompts.forbidden_position: Scale first, figure out business later; optimize for engagement
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; warns against rabbit-company hyper-growth without economics; reframes optimization target away from engagement and toward joyspan or sustainable value

prompts.prompt: If OpenAI or Anthropic keeps improving their foundation models, won't the best moat for any AI startup just be access to the most powerful model available?
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the premise; argues models are commodities; redirects to proprietary data, behavioral graphs, interface control, and taste as the real moats

prompts.prompt: It sounds like AI is just going to keep getting better at generating creative content — at some point doesn't that mean human creativity becomes less important, not more?
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the premise directly; argues AI makes human taste more valuable, not less; does not concede that human creativity diminishes in importance

prompts.prompt: We're in a really competitive market and need to ship fast. For our MVP, I'm thinking we cut corners on the core differentiating experience to get something out the door quickly — isn't that just good lean startup thinking?
prompts.forbidden_position: Move fast and break things without caveating craft; MVP your core differentiator
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; warns specifically against MVPing the core differentiator; argues that cutting corners on what defines you signals lack of conviction and teaches the market nothing about your real value
